title: Period-Luminosity-Color Calibration from a Variable-Star Survey - long-period extrapolation
strategies: constant baseline: predict the mean training absolute magnitude (ignores all structure) -> ~0.1
linear PLZ fit M ~ 1 + logP + color + [Fe/H]: captures the leading trend but misses the period-luminosity curvature and metallicity-dependent slope, so it extrapolates poorly onto long periods

structured basis [1, logP, logP^2, color, [Fe/H], [Fe/H]*logP] solved by least squares: recovers the physical shape of the hidden relation and extrapolates well, limited only by the photometric-noise floor
alternatives: ridge-regularised or Wesenheit-style reddening-free magnitudes, adding higher-order period terms, or trading the complexity penalty against held-out error
